channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Designing Posters: Turning Research into Visual Impact
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Need to create a poster for a symposium or conference? This workshop introduces key poster design principles to help you communicate your research clearly and effectively. Learn how to organize content, apply visual design strategies, and create engaging posters that connect with your audience. Registration preferred using the link.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Tabling at the Union Every Wednesday
item.description: Meet your tutors every Wednesday, 12–2 PM in the Union. Stop by the Student Success Center table to meet our tutors, grab a Puck Cookie, and book a session on the spot. We offer Drop-In Tutoring at three locations (Success Center, ECAV, and Nason), Small Group Tutoring for upper-level courses, and One-to-One Tutoring, new this semester. No appointment needed to say hi. Come find us!
item.author: Students

item.title: Student Culinary Council Meeting - March 18th
item.description: Join Rensselaer Dining on March 18th at 3:30 pm in the Student Union Room 1602 for our Student Culinary Council Meeting. Complimentary Starbucks for the first 10 students and all participants are entered into a raffle for a FREE Jimmy John’s catering package. We hope to see you there. Be sure to follow Rensselaer Dining on social media @rensselaerdining
item.author: Students

item.title: GRADUATE EDUCATION SEMINAR Scientific Publishing & Ethics in the time of Artificial Intelligence Marlene Silva, PhD Elsevier
item.description: Date: Monday, March 23, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM - Please join Marlene Silva, PhD, for the Scientific Publishing & Ethics in the Time of Artificial Intelligence workshop. We will explore the world of scientific writing and scholarly publishing to help researchers communicate their discoveries clearly, confidently, and effectively. Light refreshments will be served.
item.author: Students

item.title: Still Accepting Applications for CSTEP!
item.description: The Collegiate Science & Technology Entry Program (CSTEP) is excited to announce that applications for the Spring semester are now open! CSTEP is an academic enrichment program designed to support New York State residents who are economically disadvantaged and pursuing careers in STEM or health-related fields.
item.author: Students

item.title: Cap & Gown Information
item.description: Cap & Gown Information can be found here: https://administration.rpi.edu/collegiate-store/commencement Deadlines: Bachelor or Master Degrees: If you wish to participate in the early May pickup event order online no later than Friday, March 27, 2026. If you want the regalia shipped to you, order online no later than Thursday April 9, 2026. Doctorate Degrees: Friday, March 20, 2026 For additional questions, reach out to: RPI@bkstr.com
item.author: Students

item.title: ASCE Student Chapter Meeting
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 19, 2026 6:00 PM - 7:00 PM - McLaren Engineering Group will offer insight into their comprehensive engineering services. Join fellow ASCE members for some pizza while learning what projects McLaren is currently developing.
item.author: Students

item.title: More courses being add to Tutoring in Nason
item.description: Did you know we offer tutoring from our Success Center Tutors in Nason Hall? Check it out: -Physics I & II- Mondays, 7-9pm -CAD - Mondays, 7-9pm -CAL I & II -Tuesdays, 7-9pm -CS I and Data Structures - Wednesdays, 7-9pm
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense | Linh Tran
item.description: Date: Friday, March 20, 2026 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM - Name: Linh Tran Advisors: Ana Milanova and Stacy Patterson Day: March 20th Time: 11:00am Location: LOW 3045 or https://rensselaer.webex.com/meet/tranl3
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense | Zhenhan Huang
item.description: Date: Friday, March 20, 2026 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM - PhD Candidate: Zhenhan Huang Advisor: Prof. Jianxi Gao Date: March 20th Time: 1:00 pm Where: Webex - https://rensselaer.webex.com/rensselaer/j.php?MTID=mc82e5d5e37b65cac02faec4acfe6f507
item.author: Students

item.title: Maintenance Game
item.description: Friday / March 20, 2026 Presented by Games and Simulation Arts and Sciences Demo Peter Burr
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Team Places 4th in Lockheed Martin Ethics in Engineering Case Competition!
item.description: In February two RPI undergraduate students, Nyati Misra ’27 (BMED) and John Weller ’27 (AERO), placed in the Top 4 out of 72 schools nationwide! They represented RPI with exceptional analytical rigor, professionalism, and ethical depth. Throughout the competition, they demonstrated remarkable composure, clarity of thought, and principled decision-making, serving as outstanding ambassadors for RPI. Dr. Azita Hirsa served as their faculty advisor for this competition. To learn more about the…
item.author: Community

item.title: Gamefest 2026
item.description: March 27-28, 2026 GameFest is an annual showcase of student talent in game development, bringing together students from around New York State and the Northeast to exhibit their games. The GameFest awards recognize excellence in game design, technical innovation, artistic quality, and narrative design, and are awarded by a panel of expert judges from game studios in the Capital Region. Presented by Games and Experimental Media Symposium Annual showcase and symposium of student game development
item.author: Community

item.title: Conducting Life
item.description: Friday / March 20, 2026 Conducting Life features the evolving process of colorfully transforming microbial communities reflexively articulating Gertrude Stein’s language riddles. Presented by Department of Arts Installation Jeni Wightman
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday March 20: Carousel Digital Signage
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday March 20 from 11am-12pm, for a hands-on training session and learn how to create and publish slides on RPI’s digital signage system, Carousel Digital Signage. Whether you’re promoting a lecture, club event, or campus initiative—this session will help you get your message on screens across RPI. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: NROTC to Host Helicopter Landing on ’86 Field
item.description: On Wednesday April 1 at 11:30 AM, RPI’s Naval Reserve Officers’ Training Corps (NROTC) will host the landing and display of a U.S. Marine Corps Bell UH-1Y Venom helicopter on ’86 Field. Members of the campus community are invited to view the helicopter landing at 12 pm and meet the pilots until 2 pm. The community is also invited to observe take off between 3:30 - 4 pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: Scientists Create a New State of Matter at Room Temperature Using Light and Nanostructures
item.description: Engineering professor Wei Bao and colleagues have created a new and unusual state of matter — known as a supersolid — by engineering how light and matter interact inside a nanoscale device. The work, published in Nature Nanotechnology, demonstrates that this exotic quantum phase can exist at room temperature, overcoming a long-standing limitation in the field.
item.author: Community

item.title: Spring Town Meeting
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 3:00 PM - 4:00 PM - President Martin A. Schmidt ’81 invites you to attend the Spring Town Meeting on Wednesday, March 25 at 3 p.m. in the Curtis R. Priem Experimental Media and Performing Arts Center (EMPAC) Theater. Register at https://webforms.rpi.edu/spring-town-meeting
item.author: Community

item.title: 03/25/2026 Onboarding session for Qedma’s QESEM: error mitigation Qiskit function used with Quantum
item.description: RPI has recently acquired a license to Qedma’s QESEM: error mitigation Qiskit function for use with RPI’s dedicated IBM Quantum System One and Premium IBM Quantum Cloud account. An onboarding session has been scheduled March 25th, 9:30 AM in CII 3206. Please click in for additional details.
item.author: Community

item.title: 2026 Spring Showcase Concert
item.description: Friday / March 20, 2026 at 7:00 pm The Rensselaer Music Association will be performing in EMPAC this Friday! Our groups are excited to perform for you in this beautiful space - this brass heavy set will feature Symphonic Band, Trumpet Troupe and Analog Brainrot, the RMA’s tuba and euphonium quartet. Hope to see you there! Repertoire: Symphonic Band: Guadalcanal March (Richard Rodgers, arr. Erik Leidzen), Sea Storm (Qunicy Hilliard), Arabesque (Samuel Hazo) Presented by Rensselaer Music…
item.author: Community

item.title: Guest Speaker Deren Pulley: Is the Gut Holding You?
item.description: Deren Pulley’s dissertation explores how people persist when the gut itself – the mediator of subjective thresholds – is lost in order to live otherwise. Forever grieving the gut through techniques of the body, new forms of excremental intimacy, and mourning practices offers an ethical possibility, beyond repair, for living in the ruins of gut collapse. Join on March 18 at 11:30am, in Sage 5711.
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty & Staff Appreciation Lunch – March 18, 2026
item.description: Faculty and staff are invited to enjoy a delicious lunch for just $6 plus tax on Wednesday, March 18, 2026! Join us at Commons or Russell Sage during lunch and we hope to see you there.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop- Book Discussion: “The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People: Powerful Lessons in Personal Change”-Virtual
item.description:
Join an instructor-led discussion of Stephen R. Covey’s incredibly successful book (available in Percipio). It offers a revolutionary program to breaking the patterns of self-defeating behavior that keep us from achieving our goals and reaching our fullest potential and describes how to replace them with a principle-focused approach to problem-solving.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
